Motion For Protective Order Regarding Production Of Personal Health Information
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Cary J. Calderone of Sand Hill Law (650-906-0963) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Aron K. Liang of Philiip M. Anderson & Associates (925-225-6838) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Brendan J. Fogarty of LHB Pacific Law Partners LLP (510-841-7777) appears as counsel for State Farm

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modifications.

Plaintiff's motion for a protective order regarding production of personal health information is denied in large part. Defendant Chinakrit Piamchon's agreement to limit the subpoenas to records related to head, neck and back issues for the five years preceding the date of the subpoenas eliminates any overbreadth issue. The subpoenas are limited to those records only and the records received by defendant may be used for this litigation only and not disclosed to anyone other than for purposes of this litigation only. Plaintiff's HIPAA argument lacks merit. Counsel for defendant is required to destroy or return all of plaintiff's medical information within 6 months of the final conclusion of this matter. State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company may not distribute nor provide copies of medical records unless required by law. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Production Of Personal Health Information

Clarke B. Holland, of LHB Pacific Law Partners, LLP, Tel. 949.242.2441, for non-party State Farm.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defendant's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. Motion For Protective Order Regarding Production Of Personal Health Information is continued to September 7, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Defendant informed that a $30. Court Reporter fee will be assessed and must be paid before the hearing date. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
